name: Amazon Selling Partner
one_liner: Amazon's Selling Partner API (SP-API) lets sellers and solution providers programmatically manage orders, inventory, listings, fulfillment (FBA), finances, and reports across Amazon marketplaces.
access_notes: Requires a Professional selling account and registration in Amazon's Developer Central. Developers must submit a developer profile with security-control questionnaire, agree to the Acceptable Use / Data Protection policies, and qualify for role-based access. Public apps must be listed in the SP-API Appstore with a public website. Restricted roles handling PII require an architecture review with Amazon's Solutions Architecture team. A sandbox exists, but real API use is gated behind app review and role approval.
api_notes: Large REST API surface spanning many sections: Orders, Feeds, Reports, Catalog Items, Listings Items, FBA Inbound/Inventory/Outbound, Merchant Fulfillment, Finances, Product Pricing, Notifications, Shipping, Vendor APIs, Tokens (RDT), and more — well over 100 operations. Official Java, C#, Python, PHP, TypeScript SDKs and OpenAPI models are published on GitHub (amzn/selling-partner-api-models).
mcp_notes: No official Amazon-shipped MCP server for SP-API found. Amazon has an official Amazon Ads MCP server (separate product, ads data only). SP-API MCP servers that exist are community/third-party (e.g. mattcoatsworth/AmazonSeller-mcp-server on GitHub, Pipedream, Seller Labs, Zapier).
main_blocker: Registration/approval gate: needs a Professional Amazon seller account, developer profile review, role qualification, and (for PII roles) an architecture review before production API access is granted.

evidence.claim: SP-API registration requires a Professional selling account, a developer profile with security controls, and role-based access qualification; PII roles require an architecture review.
Since October 2, 2023 SP-API no longer requires AWS IAM or AWS Signature Version 4; authentication uses Login with Amazon (LWA) OAuth 2.0 access tokens.
Public developers must list their application in the Amazon Selling Partner Appstore and provide a public website describing the app's services.

open_questions: Exact current operation count per API section; whether the sandbox alone (pre-approval) returns useful mock data sufficient for building without full role approval.
